items.question_text: When do the overdue aircraft procedures in Section 3 (including ALNOT issuance) apply to an aircraft described as 'missing' or 'unreported'?

choices.text: The procedures also apply to aircraft referred to as missing or unreported — those terms are covered by the same section.
Only when the aircraft is on an IFR flight plan and at least 60 minutes past its ETA.
Only after the destination airport tower confirms the aircraft never arrived.
Missing and unreported aircraft are handled by the FSS only; they fall outside the Section 3 ALNOT framework.

items.explanation: Per the note to 10-3-1, the procedures in Section 3 also apply to an aircraft referred to as 'missing' or 'unreported.'

items.question_text: Which facility serves as the central point for collecting and disseminating information on an overdue or missing aircraft that is not on an IFR flight plan?

choices.text: The Flight Service Station (FSS).
The ARTCC, the same as for IFR overdue aircraft.
The terminal facility that last worked the aircraft.
The airport operator at the aircraft's point of departure.

items.explanation: Per the note to 10-3-2, FSSs serve as the central points for collecting and disseminating information on an overdue or missing aircraft which is not on an IFR flight plan. Non-FSS ATC facilities must refer calls and inquiries about these flights to the appropriate FSS.

items.question_text: How does the information forwarded to the RCC under 10-3-3 differ from the information forwarded to the ARTCC under 10-3-2?

choices.text: The RCC notification adds the facility and person calling, and the action taken by the reporting facility and proposed action.
The two lists are identical; there is no difference between the ARTCC and RCC information sets.
The RCC receives fewer details because rescue coordination needs only the flight plan and last position.
The ARTCC receives fuel status and beacon code, but the RCC does not need those items.

items.explanation: Per 10-3-2 and 10-3-3, while the lists substantially overlap, the RCC notification under 10-3-3 adds the facility and person calling (item a) and the action taken by the reporting facility and proposed action (item f).

items.question_text: Under what circumstances may an ALNOT be expanded beyond the normal 50-mile-either-side-of-route distribution?

choices.text: At the recommendation of the RCC or at the controller's discretion, the ALNOT may be issued to cover the maximum range of the aircraft.
Only if the aircraft is military and the route crosses international boundaries.
Only when the aircraft has been missing for more than 24 hours without contact.
The ALNOT cannot be expanded beyond 50 miles either side of the route under any circumstances.

items.explanation: Per 10-3-4a, at the recommendation of the RCC or at the controller's discretion, the ALNOT may be issued to cover the maximum range of the aircraft.

items.question_text: What are the three independent triggers for transferring responsibility for further search from the ARTCC to the RCC?

choices.text: 30 minutes after estimated fuel exhaustion, one hour after ALNOT issuance without locating the aircraft, or ALNOT search completed with negative results.
All three conditions must occur together — fuel exhaustion + 30 min, ALNOT + 1 hour, AND negative search results — before any transfer happens.
Only after the ALNOT has been outstanding for 24 hours, regardless of other conditions.
Only when the destination air traffic manager formally requests the transfer.

items.explanation: Per 10-3-5, any one of the three listed conditions transfers responsibility: 30 minutes after estimated fuel exhaustion, one hour after ALNOT issuance without locating the aircraft, or ALNOT search completed with negative results.

items.question_text: When providing last known position data for an SAR mission, what method is authorized to prevent undue delay if a formal data extraction would take too long?

choices.text: Use any available method to determine the initial latitude and longitude, then follow up as soon as possible with the formal coordinates.
Only the formal terminal or en route data extraction tool may be used; approximate methods are never approved.
Wait indefinitely for the formal extraction because SAR missions cannot use provisional position data.
Forward only a verbal description of the area and skip coordinates entirely until the formal extraction finishes.

items.explanation: Per 10-3-6b, if necessary to prevent undue delay, utilize any available method to determine the initial latitude and longitude, then follow up as soon as possible with the formal extraction.

items.question_text: Who directs the cancellation of an ALNOT?

choices.text: The RCC — the ALNOT is canceled when directed by the RCC after the aircraft is located or the search is abandoned.
The originating ARTCC on its own authority — the RCC is notified after the center independently decides to cancel.
The destination airport tower — only the tower that was expecting the arrival may cancel the ALNOT.
Any facility that receives an INREQ response confirming the aircraft has been located.

items.explanation: Per 10-3-7a, cancel the ALNOT when directed by the RCC when the aircraft is located or the search is abandoned.
